1) The more ME escalates, the stronger the Dollar bid gets. As soon as the BoJ gives Treasury the sign that they're ready for the final FX intervention (triggering the reverse Yen carry unwind) that's when the US hits Iranian infrastructure. That's the final blow.

#oott

2) Crude rallies, products lead the move. The squeeze doesn't end until demand destruction kicks in and the economy rolls into recession. Simultaneously, Japan pulls the plug on the carry,  equities reprice hard and that's when China makes its move across multiple fronts.

quoted_text:
HORMUZ | 8 July Crude transit capacity update 
Today's traffic is back to pre-MOU level: 1 OUTBOUND, 2 INBOUND; 3 more PG vessels surfaced in Nigeria.
148 M bbls / 97 tankers in PG, of them 47 are trapped, 50 are post MOU entries. HYA exports are at 45% pre-war level today.

NOTE: I will only flag lack of INBOUND transit capacity when Gulf approaches ~105M bbls (enough for 7 days of 15M bbls flow)
